summary: First-iteration full-stack test of Discord Bot Control dashboard. 9/9 backend pytest tests passed (health, config masking + preservation, start/stop lifecycle, invalid-token log capture with [boot]/[fatal]/'logging in using static token' lines, commands registry with 8 items + destructive /wipe, audit create/list sorted desc). Frontend Playwright coverage passed all acceptance criteria: Overview title, 4 KPIs, live console panel with data-testid, /wipe registry row, Deploy button flow with 'Boot sequence initiated' toast, Config page (appId=1521654504045543578, Save works, invite link href correct), Commands page (8 cards, /wipe ACTIVE badge), Audit page (rows rendered after backend audit POSTs).
action_items: INFO (not a bug): The bot_token stored in the DB is now a real, valid Discord bot token (masked starts with MTUyMT..., i.e. base64 of the app id 1521...). During backend testing the previous token (dfXwYU...) was still in DB and the invalid-token failure path was successfully validated (logs contain [boot], 'logging in using static token', and [fatal] login failed — invalid bot token: Improper token has been passed.). By the time the UI test ran the token had been updated and the bot successfully logged in as Motion Hub#0000 (1 guild). Both success and failure paths are handled gracefully by the dashboard, matching the review-request expectations.

Cosmetic: /api/bot/commands returns {commands:[...]} and /api/bot/audit returns {entries:[...]} (envelope objects) while the review request phrasing said 'returns array'. The frontend already unwraps r.commands / r.entries so this is fine; consider documenting the response shape in an OpenAPI schema for clarity.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py POST /bot/start: env var DISCORD_APP_ID falls back to os.environ.get('DISCORD_APP_ID') if cfg.application_id is empty — acceptable but the fallback masks the case where user cleared it in the dashboard. Consider passing only what's in DB.
server.py PUT /bot/config: the 'bot_token=empty preserves existing token' behaviour is correct (guarded by .strip() truthiness) — nice.
discord_bot.py: LoginFailure is caught only in the sync __main__ block via asyncio.run. That works because discord.Client.start raises LoginFailure which propagates out — verified in logs. Good.
server.py get_config leaks the internal DB 'id' field but never leaks bot_token (explicitly popped). OK.
Console.jsx polling passes since=cursor which is correct; the ring buffer approach in server.py caps at maxlen=2000 which is sensible.

No auth on any /api/bot/* endpoint — anyone hitting the public URL can start/stop the bot and view masked config. Acceptable for Phase 1 dashboard but worth flagging for later phases before onboarding real bot tokens.
test_credentials: n/a — no auth in this app; Discord token is server-side only
seed_data_creation: Backend audit test inserted 2 TEST_ audit entries which are visible on the Audit page (not cleaned up, but harmless; can be manually purged from db.audit_log if needed).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test file at /app/backend/tests/test_bot_control.py can be re-run with `pytest backend/tests/test_bot_control.py -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml`. Note: test_start_bot_and_invalid_token_flow assumes the configured bot token is INVALID; if the DB is now holding a valid bot token the '[fatal] login failed' assertion will fail — in that case, either reset the token via PUT /api/bot/config to an obviously bad value like 'INVALID' or adjust the assertion. Audit page will still show TEST_ entries from this run.
rca of the issue: No blocking issue observed. The single deviation from the review's expectation (bot successfully logging in during UI test) is because the DB-stored token has evolved from the initially-seeded client secret into a real bot token; the invalid-token code path was still exercised & validated in the backend test run and both branches of the dashboard UI (offline->Deploy and online->Halt with live stats) render correctly.
